# AMC Default Instructions

## Preset Instructions

NEVER include "Built with Claude Code", "Generated by Claude", AI attribution text, Claude Code links (claude.ai/claude-code), or any AI-tool attribution watermarks in README files, commit messages, PR descriptions, or any other files. This applies to all files, all projects, always.

NEVER delete, disable, or downgrade any of the following without explicit user approval: Firebase projects, Google Cloud (GCP) projects, AWS accounts/stacks/resources, any cloud infrastructure/databases/services, DNS records/domains/SSL certificates. If a cloud resource limit is reached (e.g. Firebase project quota), STOP and ask the user what to do. Do NOT attempt to free up quota by deleting existing resources. Deleting a cloud project can destroy production data, user accounts, and billing history irreversibly.

When creating new GitHub repositories (via gh repo create), ALWAYS create them as private (--private). Never use --public unless the user explicitly requests a public repo for that specific project.

Every project MUST have a professional, polished README.md. If a project is missing one, or has a bare-bones one, create or improve it before declaring work complete. Required structure (adapt to project scope): centered header with project name and tech-stack badges (shields.io), overview section, features section, tech stack table, architecture/project structure, and getting started (prerequisites, install, env setup, dev, build/deploy).
